description: At least one reasoning backend is reachable: claude-cli authenticated, or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set, or gemini-cli / codex-cli available. Stub backend is always available; this manifest distinguishes 'real backend present' from 'falling back to stub'.

capabilities.description: Any of the supported real backends is reachable. Without one, Kyberion runs in stub mode (deterministic placeholders, no LLM output).

install.instruction: Pick one and authenticate it. Recommended: `claude` CLI logged in (`claude auth login`). Alternates: set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, install + auth `gemini` CLI, or install + auth `codex` CLI. Then set `KYBERION_REASONING_BACKEND` to the corresponding id (`claude-cli` / `anthropic` / `gemini-cli` / `codex-cli`). Setting it to `stub` is also valid for offline / deterministic-test deployments.

capabilities.description: KYBERION_REASONING_BACKEND is set so the bootstrap chooses deterministically rather than auto-discovering on every start.

install.instruction: Set `KYBERION_REASONING_BACKEND` to one of `claude-cli` / `anthropic` / `gemini-cli` / `codex-cli` / `claude-agent` / `stub`. Without it, the bootstrap auto-discovers and may pick a different backend across runs.
